title: Knight's Tour Sudoku Backtracking with Edge Cases and Isolated Diagonals

statement.markdown: You are given a 64-cell square board, representing a 8x8 Sudoku grid. Place 8 Knights in the grid such that each Knight is separated by at least one empty cell and does not attack any other Knight. The goal is to place the Knights in a way that each cell has a Knight or is empty. There are four possible Knight placements on an 8x8 board: [1, 2], [1, -2], [-1, 2], and [-1, -2].
statement.inputFormat: The board size will be 64x64 cells.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be True if a valid placement of the 8 Knights is found, False otherwise.
statement.notes: You may assume that the board is initially empty.

examples.input: The board size and cell position

hints.text: Try using a backtracking algorithm with a depth-first search approach.
Be sure to handle edge cases such as an empty board or invalid placements.

editorial.title: Editorial: Knight's Tour Sudoku Backtracking with Edge Cases and Isolated Diagonals
editorial.intuition: Start by choosing a placement strategy, then use a combination of backtracking and depth-first search to fill in the remaining cells.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try all possible placements for each Knight on the board.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a backtracking algorithm with a more efficient depth-first search approach.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a backtracking algorithm with a depth-first search approach and additional optimizations such as pruning the search space based on the current placement.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The backtracking algorithm guarantees a valid placement if one exists, but may return False if no valid placement is found.
editorial.dryRun: Choose a start position for the first Knight and try to place the remaining Knights.
